explicit_text: I was born in Virginia City on July 17, 1920, specifically on A Street, which we jokingly called Ophir Heights. This area was on the Ophir mine property up on the hill, and we used the term 'heights' because it was where people of means lived. Growing up there, I was surrounded by mining history and remnants like glory holes and small shafts.

explicit_text: The Ophir Mine was a significant part of my childhood environment in Virginia City. Although the last shaft was about three blocks down the hill from my home, the mine property extended up to where we lived. We even named our neighborhood Ophir Heights because it was on the Ophir mine property, and mining activity shaped much of the area.

explicit_text: My grandfather, Neil Gallagher, came from Ireland during the time of the Great Famine. explicit_text: The Yellow Jacket Mine was the site of a tragic accident in 1880 where my grandfather, Neil Gallagher, was killed. He was working as a shaft man when a cage carrying steel tipped over and fell onto men below, killing six out of eight workers, including him. This accident deeply affected my family and shaped my father's early life.
